title: Respiratory PFTs: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Respiratory PFTs
Identify key diagnostic features of Respiratory PFTs
Apply clinical reasoning to Respiratory PFTs management
Recognize common pitfalls in Respiratory PFTs

sections.title: Overview of Respiratory PFTs
sections.content:
**Respiratory PFTs**

FEV1/FVC ratio, obstructive vs restrictive patterns, DLCO interpretation, flow-volume loops

**Key Points:**
• Obstructive: FEV1/FVC <0.70 (COPD, asthma); air trapping → increased TLC, RV
• Restrictive: FEV1/FVC normal or increased, but reduced FVC and TLC (ILD, chest wall disease, neuromuscular)
• Reversibility: >12% AND >200mL increase in FEV1 post-bronchodilator = significant (supports asthma)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Obstructive: FEV1/FVC <0.70 (COPD, asthma); air trapping → increased TLC, RV
• Restrictive: FEV1/FVC normal or increased, but reduced FVC and TLC (ILD, chest wall disease, neuromuscular)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Reversibility: >12% AND >200mL increase in FEV1 post-bronchodilator = significant (supports asthma)
• DLCO decreased: emphysema, ILD, pulmonary HTN, anemia; DLCO increased: polycythemia, pulmonary hemorrhage, asthma
• Flow-volume loops: variable intrathoracic obstruction (flattened expiratory), variable extrathoracic (flattened inspiratory), fixed (both flattened)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Respiratory PFTs:**

• Reversibility: >12% AND >200mL increase in FEV1 post-bronchodilator = significant (supports asthma)
• DLCO decreased: emphysema, ILD, pulmonary HTN, anemia; DLCO increased: polycythemia, pulmonary hemorrhage, asthma
• Flow-volume loops: variable intrathoracic obstruction (flattened expiratory), variable extrathoracic (flattened inspiratory), fixed (both flattened)

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Respiratory PFTs?

options.text: Obstructive: FEV1/FVC <0.70 (COPD, asthma); air trapping → increased TLC, RV
options.explanation: Obstructive: FEV1/FVC <0.70 (COPD, asthma); air trapping → increased TLC, RV

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Respiratory PFTs?

options.text: Flow-volume loops: variable intrathoracic obstruction (flattened expiratory), variable extrathoracic (flattened inspiratory), fixed (both flattened)
options.explanation: Flow-volume loops: variable intrathoracic obstruction (flattened expiratory), variable extrathoracic (flattened inspiratory), fixed (both flattened)

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Respiratory PFTs. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Restrictive: FEV1/FVC normal or increased, but reduced FVC and TLC (ILD, chest wall disease, neuromuscular)
options.explanation: Restrictive: FEV1/FVC normal or increased, but reduced FVC and TLC (ILD, chest wall disease, neuromuscular)

mcqs.stem: Pulmonary function tests show: FEV1 55% predicted, FVC 90% predicted, FEV1/FVC ratio 0.61, significant bronchodilator response (+15% FEV1). What is the interpretation?

options.text: Moderate obstructive lung disease with reversibility, consistent with asthma
options.explanation: FEV1/FVC <0.70 confirms obstruction. FEV1 50-79% predicted = moderate severity (GOLD/GINA). Significant bronchodilator response (≥12% AND ≥200mL improvement in FEV1) suggests reversible airway obstruction characteristic of asthma. COPD typically shows less reversibility.

options.text: Restrictive lung disease based on reduced FEV1
options.explanation: Restrictive disease shows reduced FVC with PRESERVED or elevated FEV1/FVC ratio (≥0.70). Here, FVC is 90% (normal) and FEV1/FVC is 0.61 (reduced), indicating obstruction, not restriction.

options.text: Normal spirometry — FVC is within normal limits
options.explanation: Normal FVC does not rule out obstructive disease. The FEV1/FVC ratio of 0.61 (<0.70 threshold) definitively identifies airflow obstruction.

options.text: Fixed airway obstruction consistent with COPD
options.explanation: A 15% bronchodilator response indicates significant REVERSIBILITY, which argues against fixed obstruction. COPD typically shows <12% improvement post-bronchodilator.
